title: OpenTelemetry Collector Contrib config (v0.152.0)
description: OSS Collector with the full contrib component set. JSON Schema for the configuration of OpenTelemetry Collector Contrib version 0.152.0, generated from the distribution's manifest.yaml.

^nop(/.+)?$.description: Discards all data; useful for tests.

^otlp(/.+)?$.description: Config defines configuration for the OTLP receiver — accepts OTLP data over gRPC and/or HTTP.

protocols.description: One or both of `grpc` and `http`. At least one must be configured.

grpc.description: gRPC server config. Empty object enables defaults (listen on 0.0.0.0:4317).

endpoint.description: Address to listen on. Default 0.0.0.0:4317.

max_recv_msg_size_mib.description: Maximum receive size in MiB. Default 4.

http.description: HTTP server config. Empty object enables defaults (listen on 0.0.0.0:4318).

endpoint.description: Address to listen on. Default 0.0.0.0:4318.

traces_url_path.description: Override the OTLP traces path. Default /v1/traces.

metrics_url_path.description: Override the OTLP metrics path. Default /v1/metrics.

logs_url_path.description: Override the OTLP logs path. Default /v1/logs.

^active_directory_ds(/.+)?$.description: The following settings are optional: - `metrics` (default: see [`DefaultMetricsSettings`](./internal/metadata/generated_metrics.go)): Allows enabling and disabling specific metrics from being collected in this receiver. - `collection_interval` (default = `10s`): The interval at which metrics are emitted by this receiver. - `initial_delay` (default = `1s`): defines how long this receiver waits before starting.

^apache(/.+)?$.description: This receiver supports Apache Web Server version 2.4.13+.

^apachespark(/.+)?$.description: Config defines the configuration for the various elements of the receiver agent.

^awscloudwatch(/.+)?$.description: Config is the overall config structure for the awscloudwatchreceiver

autodiscover_config.description: AutodiscoverConfig is the configuration for the autodiscovery functionality of log groups

group_config.description: GroupConfig is the configuration for log group collection

logs_config.description: LogsConfig is the configuration for the logs portion of this receiver

stream_config.description: StreamConfig represents the configuration for the log stream filtering

^awscontainerinsightreceiver(/.+)?$.description: Config defines configuration for aws ecs container metrics receiver.

add_full_pod_name_metric_label.description: The "FullPodName" attribute is the pod name including suffix If false FullPodName label is not added The default value is false

add_service_as_attribute.description: Whether to add the associated service name as attribute. The default is true

collection_interval.description: CollectionInterval is the interval at which metrics should be collected. The default is 60 second.

container_orchestrator.description: ContainerOrchestrator is the type of container orchestration service, e.g. eks or ecs. The default is eks.

prefer_full_pod_name.description: The "PodName" attribute is set based on the name of the relevant controllers like Daemonset, Job, ReplicaSet, ReplicationController, ... If it cannot be set that way and PrefFullPodName is true, the "PodName" attribute is set to the pod's own name. The default value is false

^awsecscontainermetrics(/.+)?$.description: Config defines configuration for aws ecs container metrics receiver.

collection_interval.description: CollectionInterval is the interval at which metrics should be collected

access_key.description: AccessKey is checked against the one received with each request. This can be set when creating or updating the Firehose delivery stream.

encoding.description: Encoding identifies the encoding of records received from Firehose. Defaults to telemetry-specific encodings: "cwlog" for logs, and "cwmetrics" for metrics. This default behavior is deprecated as of v0.149.0, and in the future it will be required to specify the encoding explicitly.

record_type.description: RecordType is an alias for Encoding for backwards compatibility. It is an error to specify both encoding and record_type. Deprecated: [v0.121.0] use Encoding instead.

^awslambda(/.+)?$.description: A receiver for collecting logs & metrics from AWS services via Lambda invocations. AWS Lambda is a popular serverless service used extensively for event-driven architectures. Many AWS services (S3, CloudWatch, SNS, SQS) can trigger Lambda functions, making it an ideal entry point for collecting data from AWS services. This receiver is designed to run as part of an OpenTelemetry Collector deployed as an AWS Lambda function.

cloudwatch.description: CloudWatch defines configuration options for CloudWatch Lambda trigger.

encoding.description: Encoding defines the encoding to decode incoming Lambda invocation data. This extension is expected to further process content of the events that are extracted from Lambda trigger. If receiving data is in different formats(ex:- a mix of VPC flow logs, CloudTrail logs), receiver is recommended to have separate Lambda functions with specific extension configurations.

failure_bucket_arn.description: FailureBucketARN is the ARN of receiver deployment Lambda's error destination.

s3.description: S3 defines configuration options for S3 Lambda trigger.

encoding.description: Encoding defines the encoding to decode incoming Lambda invocation data. This extension is expected to further process content of the events that are extracted from Lambda trigger. If receiving data is in different formats(ex:- a mix of VPC flow logs, CloudTrail logs), receiver is recommended to have separate Lambda functions with specific extension configurations.

^awss3(/.+)?$.description: Config defines the configuration for the file receiver.

encoding.description: Encoding defines the encoding configuration for the file receiver.

s_3_downloader_config.description: S3DownloaderConfig contains aws s3 downloader related config to controls things like bucket, prefix, batching, connections, retries, etc.

sqs_config.description: SQSConfig holds SQS queue configuration for receiving object change notifications.

endpoint.description: Endpoint is the optional custom endpoint for SQS (useful for testing).

max_number_of_messages.description: MaxNumberOfMessages specifies the maximum number of messages to receive in a single poll. Valid values: 1-10. Default is 10.

queue_url.description: QueueURL is the URL of the SQS queue to receive S3 notifications.

region.description: Region specifies the AWS region of the SQS queue.

wait_time_seconds.description: WaitTimeSeconds specifies the duration (in seconds) for long polling SQS messages. Maximum is 20 seconds. Default is 20 seconds.

sqs.description: SQS configures receiving S3 object change notifications via an SQS queue.

^awsxray(/.+)?$.description: Config defines the configurations for an AWS X-Ray receiver.

proxy_server.description: ProxyServer defines configurations related to the local TCP proxy server.

^azure_blob(/.+)?$.description: This receiver reads logs and trace data from [Azure Blob Storage](https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/products/storage/blobs/).

endpoint.description: Azure Event Hub endpoint triggering on the `Blob Create` event

container_name.description: Name of the blob container with the logs (default = "logs")

client_id.description: Client ID, used with Service Principal authentication

client_secret.description: Client secret, used with Service Principal authentication

tenant_id.description: Tenant ID, used with Service Principal authentication

container_name.description: Name of the blob container with the traces (default = "traces")

cloud.description: Azure Cloud to authenticate against, used with Service Principal authentication

connection_string.description: Azure Blob Storage connection key, which can be found in the Azure Blob Storage resource on the Azure Portal. (no default)

event_hub.description: Configurations of Azure Event Hub triggering on the `Blob Create` event

service_principal.description: Configuration for the Service Principal credentials

storage_account_url.description: Storage Account URL, used with Service Principal authentication

^azureblob(/.+)?$.description: This receiver reads logs and trace data from [Azure Blob Storage](https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/products/storage/blobs/).

endpoint.description: Azure Event Hub endpoint triggering on the `Blob Create` event

container_name.description: Name of the blob container with the logs (default = "logs")

client_id.description: Client ID, used with Service Principal authentication

client_secret.description: Client secret, used with Service Principal authentication

tenant_id.description: Tenant ID, used with Service Principal authentication

container_name.description: Name of the blob container with the traces (default = "traces")

cloud.description: Azure Cloud to authenticate against, used with Service Principal authentication

connection_string.description: Azure Blob Storage connection key, which can be found in the Azure Blob Storage resource on the Azure Portal. (no default)

event_hub.description: Configurations of Azure Event Hub triggering on the `Blob Create` event

service_principal.description: Configuration for the Service Principal credentials

storage_account_url.description: Storage Account URL, used with Service Principal authentication

^azure_event_hub(/.+)?$.description: Azure resources and services can be [configured](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/diagnostic-settings) to send their telemetry to an Azure Event Hub. The Azure Event Hub receiver pulls telemetry from an Azure Event Hub, transforms them, and pushes them through the collector pipeline.

blob_checkpoint_store_config.description: BlobCheckpointStoreConfig defines the configuration for Azure Blob Storage based checkpoint coordination used in distributed consumption mode.

connection.description: Connection is the connection string for Azure Blob Storage. Required when the parent config does not use auth.

container_name.description: ContainerName is the blob container used for checkpoint data. Required.

storage_account_url.description: StorageAccountURL is the blob service URL (e.g., https://myaccount.blob.core.windows.net). Required when the parent config uses auth.

event_hub_config.description: EventHubConfig defines the configuration for an Azure Event Hub when using authentication.

name.description: Name is the name of the Event Hub.

namespace.description: Namespace is the fully qualified namespace of the Event Hub.

blob_checkpoint_store.description: BlobCheckpointStore enables distributed consumption using Azure Blob Storage for checkpoint coordination. When configured, the receiver uses the Azure SDK Processor for dynamic partition assignment across multiple collector instances.

^azureeventhub(/.+)?$.description: Azure resources and services can be [configured](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/diagnostic-settings) to send their telemetry to an Azure Event Hub. The Azure Event Hub receiver pulls telemetry from an Azure Event Hub, transforms them, and pushes them through the collector pipeline.

blob_checkpoint_store_config.description: BlobCheckpointStoreConfig defines the configuration for Azure Blob Storage based checkpoint coordination used in distributed consumption mode.

connection.description: Connection is the connection string for Azure Blob Storage. Required when the parent config does not use auth.

container_name.description: ContainerName is the blob container used for checkpoint data. Required.

storage_account_url.description: StorageAccountURL is the blob service URL (e.g., https://myaccount.blob.core.windows.net). Required when the parent config uses auth.

event_hub_config.description: EventHubConfig defines the configuration for an Azure Event Hub when using authentication.

name.description: Name is the name of the Event Hub.

namespace.description: Namespace is the fully qualified namespace of the Event Hub.

blob_checkpoint_store.description: BlobCheckpointStore enables distributed consumption using Azure Blob Storage for checkpoint coordination. When configured, the receiver uses the Azure SDK Processor for dynamic partition assignment across multiple collector instances.

^azure_monitor(/.+)?$.description: Config defines the configuration for the various elements of the receiver agent.

auth_config.description: AuthConfig defines the auth settings for the azure receiver.

authenticator.description: AuthenticatorID specifies the name of the azure extension to authenticate the requests to azure monitor.

auth.description: Authentication accepts the component azureauthextension, and uses it to get an access token to make requests. Using this, `credentials` and any related fields become useless.

^azuremonitor(/.+)?$.description: Config defines the configuration for the various elements of the receiver agent.

auth_config.description: AuthConfig defines the auth settings for the azure receiver.

authenticator.description: AuthenticatorID specifies the name of the azure extension to authenticate the requests to azure monitor.

auth.description: Authentication accepts the component azureauthextension, and uses it to get an access token to make requests. Using this, `credentials` and any related fields become useless.

^carbon(/.+)?$.description: Config defines configuration for the Carbon receiver.

parser.description: Parser specifies a parser and the respective configuration to be used by the receiver.

tcp_idle_timeout.description: TCPIdleTimeout is the timeout for idle TCP connections, it is ignored if transport being used is UDP.

^chrony(/.+)?$.description: The [chrony] receiver is a pure go implementation of the command `chronyc tracking` to allow for portability across systems and platforms. All of the data that would typically be captured by the tracking command is made available in this receiver, see [documentation](./documentation.md) for more details.

endpoint.description: Endpoint is the published address or unix socket that allows clients to connect to: The allowed format is: unix:///path/to/chronyd/unix.sock udp://localhost:323 The default value is unix:///var/run/chrony/chronyd.sock

^cisco_os(/.+)?$.description: Config defines configuration for Cisco OS receiver.

device_config.description: DeviceConfig represents configuration for a single Cisco device in the devices list.

devices.description: Devices is the list of Cisco devices to monitor.

^ciscoos(/.+)?$.description: Config defines configuration for Cisco OS receiver.

device_config.description: DeviceConfig represents configuration for a single Cisco device in the devices list.

devices.description: Devices is the list of Cisco devices to monitor.

^cloudflare(/.+)?$.description: Config holds all the parameters to start an HTTP server that can be sent logs from CloudFlare

max_request_body_size.description: MaxRequestBodySize sets the maximum request body size in bytes. 0 means no limit. Default: 20MB

^cloudfoundry(/.+)?$.description: Config defines configuration for Collectd receiver.

limited_client_config.description: LimitedClientConfig is a subset of ClientConfig, implemented as a separate type due to the library this configuration is used with not taking a preconfigured http.Client as input, but only taking these specific options

limited_tls_client_setting.description: LimitedTLSClientSetting is a subset of TLSClientSetting, see LimitedClientConfig for more details

^collectd(/.+)?$.description: Config defines configuration for Collectd receiver.

^couchdb(/.+)?$.description: Config defines the configuration for the various elements of the receiver agent.

^datadog(/.+)?$.description: Configuration wise is very simple, just need to specify where the Datadog receiver should listen and the read timeout.

behavior.description: Behavior sets how the `/intake` endpoint should behave. The value should be one of: `disable` (default) - disable the endpoint entirely `proxy` - proxy the requests to Datadog itself

proxy.description: Proxy controls how the `/intake` proxy operates

proxy_config.description: ProxyConfig controls how the `/intake` proxy operates

api.description: API defines the settings for calling Datadog with the proxied requests

trace_id_cache_size.description: TraceIDCacheSize sets the cache size for the 64 bits to 128 bits mapping

container_labels_to_metric_labels.description: A mapping of container label names to MetricDescriptor label keys. The corresponding container label value will become the DataPoint label value for the mapped name.  E.g. `io.kubernetes.container.name: container_spec_name` would result in a MetricDescriptor label called `container_spec_name` whose Metric DataPoints have the value of the `io.kubernetes.container.name` container label.

env_vars_to_metric_labels.description: A mapping of container environment variable names to MetricDescriptor label keys.  The corresponding env var values become the DataPoint label value. E.g. `APP_VERSION: version` would result MetricDescriptors having a label key called `version` whose DataPoint label values are the value of the `APP_VERSION` environment variable configured for that particular container, if present.

^elasticsearch(/.+)?$.description: Config is the configuration for the elasticsearch receiver

indices.description: Indices defines the indices to scrape. See https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/current/indices-stats.html#index-stats-api-path-params for which names are viable. If Indices is empty, no indices will be scraped.

nodes.description: Nodes defines the nodes to scrape. See https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/7.9/cluster.html#cluster-nodes for which selectors may be used here. If Nodes is empty, no nodes will be scraped.

password.description: Password is the password used when making REST calls to elasticsearch. Must be specified if Username is. Not required.

skip_cluster_metrics.description: SkipClusterMetrics indicates whether cluster level metrics from /_cluster/* endpoints should be scraped or not.

username.description: Username is the username used when making REST calls to elasticsearch. Must be specified if Password is. Not required.

^envoyals(/.+)?$.description: Envoy ALS (Access Log Service) is a feature of Envoy Proxy that allows for the centralized collection and management of access logs.

^expvar(/.+)?$.description: By default, without any configuration, a request will be sent to `http://localhost:8000/debug/vars` every 60 seconds. The default configuration is achieved by the following:

^faro(/.+)?$.description: Faro follows the [confighttp] configuration, some examples are shown below

^file_log(/.+)?$.description: FileLogConfig defines configuration for the file_log receiver

^filelog(/.+)?$.description: FileLogConfig defines configuration for the file_log receiver

^filestats(/.+)?$.description: - `include` (required): The glob path for files to watch - `collection_interval` (default = `1m`): The interval at which metrics are emitted by this receiver. - `initial_delay` (default = `1s`): defines how long this receiver waits before starting.

^flinkmetrics(/.+)?$.description: Config defines the configuration for the various elements of the receiver agent.

^fluentforward(/.+)?$.description: Config defines configuration for the fluentforward receiver.

endpoint.description: The address to listen on for incoming Fluent Forward events.  Should be of the form `<ip addr>:<port>` (TCP) or `unix://<socket_path>` (Unix domain socket).

^github(/.+)?$.description: Config that is exposed to this github receiver through the OTEL config.yaml

^gitlab(/.+)?$.description: Config that is exposed to this gitlab receiver through the OTEL config.yaml

include_user_attributes.description: IncludeUserAttributes controls whether user information (commit author, pipeline actor) is included Default: false (user information is excluded by default for privacy)

^googlecloudmonitoring(/.+)?$.description: This receiver gets GCP (Google Cloud Platform) metrics from [GCP Monitoring REST API] via the [Google SDK for GCP Metrics] and then convert those timeseries data into OTel Format [Pipeline Data].

metric_descriptor_filter.description: Filter for listing metric descriptors. Only support `project` and `metric.type` as filter objects. See https://cloud.google.com/monitoring/api/v3/filters#metric-descriptor-filter for more details.

^googlecloudpubsub(/.+)?$.description: The following configuration options are supported:

flow_control_config.description: FlowControlConfig defines the flow control configuration for the receiver. This is used to tune the internal flow control implementation, along with the Pub/Sub flow control settings documented at https://cloud.google.com/pubsub/docs/flow-control and https://cloud.google.com/pubsub/docs/reference/rpc/google.pubsub.v1#streamingpullrequest

max_outstanding_bytes.description: Pub/Sub flow control settings for the maximum number of outstanding bytes.

max_outstanding_messages.description: Pub/Sub flow control settings for the maximum number of outstanding messages.

stream_ack_deadline.description: The ack deadline to use for the Pub/Sub stream.

trigger_ack_batch_duration.description: The maximum duration the acknowledgement loop waits before sending the acknowledgements.

client_id.description: The client id that will be used by Pubsub to make load balancing decisions

compression.description: Lock down the compression of the payload, leave empty for attribute based detection

encoding.description: Lock down the encoding of the payload, leave empty for attribute based detection

endpoint.description: Override of the Pubsub Endpoint, leave empty for the default endpoint

ignore_encoding_error.description: Ignore errors when the configured encoder fails to decoding a PubSub messages

insecure.description: Only has effect if Endpoint is not ""

project.description: Google Cloud Project ID where the Pubsub client will connect to

subscription.description: The fully qualified resource name of the Pubsub subscription

user_agent.description: User agent that will be used by the Pubsub client to connect to the service

^googlecloudspanner(/.+)?$.description: Google Cloud Spanner enable you to investigate issues with your database by exposing via [Total and Top N built-in tables](https://cloud.google.com/spanner/docs/introspection): - Query statistics - Read statistics - Transaction statistics - Lock statistics - and others

^haproxy(/.+)?$.description: Path to the endpoint exposed by HAProxy for communications. It can be a local file socket or a HTTP URL.

^host_metrics(/.+)?$.description: Config defines configuration for HostMetrics receiver.

metadata_collection_interval.description: Collection interval for metadata. Metadata of the particular entity is collected when the entity changes. In addition metadata of all entities is collected periodically even if no changes happen. Setting the duration to 0 will disable periodic collection (however will not impact metadata collection on changes).

root_path.description: RootPath is the host's root directory (linux only).

^hostmetrics(/.+)?$.description: Config defines configuration for HostMetrics receiver.

metadata_collection_interval.description: Collection interval for metadata. Metadata of the particular entity is collected when the entity changes. In addition metadata of all entities is collected periodically even if no changes happen. Setting the duration to 0 will disable periodic collection (however will not impact metadata collection on changes).

root_path.description: RootPath is the host's root directory (linux only).

^http_check(/.+)?$.description: Config defines the configuration for the various elements of the receiver agent.

^httpcheck(/.+)?$.description: Config defines the configuration for the various elements of the receiver agent.

^influxdb(/.+)?$.description: Config defines configuration for the InfluxDB receiver.

^iis(/.+)?$.description: Config defines configuration for simple prometheus receiver.

^jaeger(/.+)?$.description: Config defines configuration for Jaeger receiver.

protocol_udp.description: ProtocolUDP is the configuration for a UDP protocol.

protocols.description: Protocols is the configuration for the supported protocols.

remote_sampling_config.description: RemoteSamplingConfig defines config key for remote sampling fetch endpoint

server_config_udp.description: ServerConfigUDP is the server configuration for a UDP protocol.

^jmx(/.+)?$.description: The `jmxreceiver` component is deprecated. Running Java as part of the Collector introduces significant operational complexity, especially since the Collector is commonly deployed as a container and official images do not include a Java runtime. This results in a broken user experience.

additional_jars.description: Array of additional JARs to be added to the class path when launching the JMX Metric Gatherer JAR Supported by: jmx-scraper and jmx-metric-gatherer

endpoint.description: The Service URL or host:port for the target coerced to one of form: service:jmx:rmi:///jndi/rmi://<host>:<port>/jmxrmi. Supported by: jmx-scraper and jmx-metric-gatherer

jar_path.description: The path for the JMX Metric Gatherer or JMX Scraper JAR (/opt/opentelemetry-java-contrib-jmx-metrics.jar by default). Supported by: jmx-scraper and jmx-metric-gatherer

jmx_configs.description: Comma-separated list of paths to custom YAML metrics definition, mandatory when TargetSystem is not set. Supported by: jmx-scraper

keystore_password.description: The keystore password for SSL Supported by: jmx-scraper and jmx-metric-gatherer

keystore_path.description: The keystore path for SSL Supported by: jmx-scraper and jmx-metric-gatherer

keystore_type.description: The keystore type for SSL Supported by: jmx-scraper and jmx-metric-gatherer

log_level.description: Log level used by the JMX metric gatherer. Should be one of: `"trace"`, `"debug"`, `"info"`, `"warn"`, `"error"`, `"off"` Supported by: jmx-metric-gatherer

otlp.description: The OTLP exporter settings Supported by: jmx-scraper and jmx-metric-gatherer

endpoint.description: The OTLP Receiver endpoint to send metrics to ("0.0.0.0:<random open port>" by default).

headers.description: The headers to include in OTLP metric submission requests.

password.description: The JMX password Supported by: jmx-scraper and jmx-metric-gatherer

realm.description: The SASL/DIGEST-MD5 realm Supported by: jmx-scraper and jmx-metric-gatherer

remote_profile.description: The JMX remote profile.  Should be one of: `"SASL/PLAIN"`, `"SASL/DIGEST-MD5"`, `"SASL/CRAM-MD5"`, `"TLS SASL/PLAIN"`, `"TLS SASL/DIGEST-MD5"`, or `"TLS SASL/CRAM-MD5"`, though no enforcement is applied. Supported by: jmx-scraper and jmx-metric-gatherer

resource_attributes.description: Map of resource attributes used by the Java SDK Autoconfigure to set resource attributes Supported by: jmx-scraper and jmx-metric-gatherer

target_source.description: The target source of metric definitions to use for the target system. Supported values are: auto, instrumentation and legacy. Supported by: jmx-scraper

target_system.description: Comma-separated list of systems to monitor Supported by: jmx-scraper and jmx-metric-gatherer

truststore_password.description: The truststore password for SSL Supported by: jmx-scraper and jmx-metric-gatherer

truststore_path.description: The truststore path for SSL Supported by: jmx-scraper and jmx-metric-gatherer

truststore_type.description: The truststore type for SSL Supported by: jmx-scraper and jmx-metric-gatherer

username.description: The JMX username Supported by: jmx-scraper and jmx-metric-gatherer

^journald(/.+)?$.description: JournaldConfig defines configuration for the journald receiver

^k8s_cluster(/.+)?$.description: Config defines configuration for kubernetes cluster receiver.

allocatable_types_to_report.description: Allocate resource types to report. See all resource types, see here: https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/administer-cluster/reserve-compute-resources/#node-allocatable

distribution.description: Whether OpenShift support should be enabled or not.

k8s_leader_elector.description: K8sLeaderElector defines the reference to the k8s leader elector extension use this when k8s cluster receiver needs to be deployed in HA mode

metadata_collection_interval.description: Collection interval for metadata. Metadata of the particular entity in the cluster is collected when the entity changes. In addition metadata of all entities is collected periodically even if no changes happen. Setting the duration to 0 will disable periodic collection (however will not impact metadata collection on changes).

metadata_exporters.description: List of exporters to which metadata from this receiver should be forwarded to.

namespace.description: Deprecated: This field is no longer supported, use cfg.Namespaces instead.

namespaces.description: Namespaces to fetch resources from. If this is set, certain cluster-wide resources such as Nodes or Namespaces will not be able to be observed. Setting this option is recommended in environments where due to security restrictions the collector cannot be granted cluster-wide permissions.

node_conditions_to_report.description: Node condition types to report. See all condition types, see here: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/architecture/nodes/#condition.

^k8s_events(/.+)?$.description: Config defines configuration for kubernetes events receiver.

namespaces.description: List of ‘namespaces’ to collect events from.

^k8sobjects(/.+)?$.description: Currently this receiver supports authentication via service accounts only. See [example](#example) for more information.

^kafkametrics(/.+)?$.description: Config represents user settings for kafkametrics receiver

cluster_alias.description: Alias name of the kafka cluster

group_match.description: GroupMatch consumer groups to collect on

refresh_frequency.description: Cluster metadata refresh frequency Configures the refresh frequency to update cached cluster metadata Defaults to 10 minutes from Sarama library If Metadata.RefreshInterval is set, this will be ignored. Deprecated [v0.122.0]: use Metadata.RefreshInterval instead.

scrapers.description: Scrapers defines which metric data points to be captured from kafka

topic_match.description: TopicMatch topics to collect metrics on

^kafka(/.+)?$.description: Config defines configuration for Kafka receiver.

after.description: If true, the messages are marked after the pipeline execution

on_error.description: If false, only the successfully processed messages are marked. This only applies to non-permanent errors. It has no impact if After is set to false. Note: this can block the entire partition in case a message processing returns a non-permanent error.

on_permanent_error.description: If false, only the successfully processed messages are marked. This only applies to permanent errors. It has no impact if After is set to false. Default value inherits from OnError for backward compatibility. Note: this can block the entire partition in case a message processing returns a permanent error.

metric_config.description: MetricConfig provides common config for a particular metric.

metrics_config.description: MetricsConfig provides config for optional receiver metrics.

kafka_receiver_records_delay.description: KafkaReceiverRecordsDelay controls whether the metric kafka_receiver_records_delay that measures the time in seconds between producing and receiving a batch of records will be reported or not. This metric is not reported by default because it may slow down high-volume consuming.

topic_encoding_config.description: TopicEncodingConfig holds signal-specific topic and encoding configuration.

encoding.description: Encoding holds the expected encoding of messages for the signal type Defaults to "otlp_proto".

exclude_topics.description: Optional exclude topics option, used only in regex mode.

topics.description: Topics holds the name of the Kafka topics from which messages of the signal type should be consumed. The default depends on the signal type: - "otlp_spans" for traces - "otlp_metrics" for metrics - "otlp_logs" for logs - "otlp_profiles" for profiles

error_backoff.description: ErrorBackoff controls backoff/retry behavior when the next consumer returns an error.

header_extraction.description: HeaderExtraction controls extraction of headers from Kafka records.

logs.description: Logs holds configuration about how logs should be consumed.

message_marking.description: MessageMarking controls the way the messages are marked as consumed.

metrics.description: Metrics holds configuration about how metrics should be consumed.

profiles.description: Profiles holds configuration about how profiles should be consumed.

traces.description: Traces holds configuration about how traces should be consumed.

^kubeletstats(/.+)?$.description: Details about the metrics produced by this receiver can be found in [metadata.yaml](./metadata.yaml) with further documentation in [documentation.md](./documentation.md)

collect_all_network_interfaces.description: NetworkCollectAllInterfaces allows to enable collecting metrics from all network interfaces instead of default one Can be set separately for Pod and Node network metrics

extra_metadata_labels.description: ExtraMetadataLabels contains list of extra metadata that should be taken from /pods endpoint and put as extra labels on metrics resource. No additional metadata is fetched by default, so there are no extra calls to /pods endpoint. Supported values include container.id and k8s.volume.type.

k8s_api_config.description: Configuration of the Kubernetes API client.

metric_groups.description: MetricGroupsToCollect provides a list of metrics groups to collect metrics from. "container", "pod", "node" and "volume" are the only valid groups.

node.description: NodeName is the node name to limit the discovery of nodes. For example, node name can be set using the downward API inside the collector pod spec as follows: env: - name: K8S_NODE_NAME valueFrom: fieldRef: fieldPath: spec.nodeName Then set this value to ${env:K8S_NODE_NAME} in the configuration.

^libhoney(/.+)?$.description: Config represents the receiver config settings within the collector's config.yaml

http_config.description: HTTPConfig defines the configuration for the HTTP server receiving traces.

traces_url_paths.description: The URL path to receive traces on. If omitted "/" will be used.

^loki(/.+)?$.description: Config defines configuration for the lokireceiver receiver.

protocols.description: Protocols is the configuration for the supported protocols.

^macos_unified_logging(/.+)?$.description: Config defines configuration for the macOS unified logging receiver Separated into a common file that isn't platform specific so that factory_others.go can reference it

archive_path.description: ArchivePath is a path or glob pattern to .logarchive directory(ies) If empty, reads from the live system logs Supports glob patterns (e.g., "*.logarchive", "**/logs/*.logarchive")

end_time.description: EndTime specifies when to stop reading logs Only used with archive_path

format.description: Format specifies the output format from the log command Options: "default" (system default), "ndjson", "json", "syslog", "compact" Default: "default"

max_log_age.description: MaxLogAge specifies the maximum age of logs to read on startup Only applies to live mode. Format: "24h", "1h30m", etc.

max_poll_interval.description: MaxPollInterval specifies the maximum interval between polling for new logs (live mode only) The actual poll interval uses exponential backoff based on whether logs are being actively written

predicate.description: Predicate is a filter predicate to pass to the log command Example: "subsystem == 'com.apple.systempreferences'"

start_time.description: StartTime specifies when to start reading logs from Format: "2006-01-02 15:04:05"

^macosunifiedlogging(/.+)?$.description: Config defines configuration for the macOS unified logging receiver Separated into a common file that isn't platform specific so that factory_others.go can reference it
