end_time.description: EndTime specifies when to stop reading logs Only used with archive_path

format.description: Format specifies the output format from the log command Options: "default" (system default), "ndjson", "json", "syslog", "compact" Default: "default"

max_log_age.description: MaxLogAge specifies the maximum age of logs to read on startup Only applies to live mode. Format: "24h", "1h30m", etc.

max_poll_interval.description: MaxPollInterval specifies the maximum interval between polling for new logs (live mode only) The actual poll interval uses exponential backoff based on whether logs are being actively written

predicate.description: Predicate is a filter predicate to pass to the log command Example: "subsystem == 'com.apple.systempreferences'"

start_time.description: StartTime specifies when to start reading logs from Format: "2006-01-02 15:04:05"

^mongodb_atlas(/.+)?$.description: The MongoDB Atlas receiver takes the following parameters. `public_key` and `private_key` are the only two required values to receive metrics and logs and are obtained via the "API Keys" tab of the MongoDB Atlas Project Access Manager. In the example below both values are being pulled from the environment.

projects.description: these parameters are only relevant in retrieval mode

events_config.description: EventsConfig is the configuration options for events collection

^mongodbatlas(/.+)?$.description: The MongoDB Atlas receiver takes the following parameters. `public_key` and `private_key` are the only two required values to receive metrics and logs and are obtained via the "API Keys" tab of the MongoDB Atlas Project Access Manager. In the example below both values are being pulled from the environment.

projects.description: these parameters are only relevant in retrieval mode

events_config.description: EventsConfig is the configuration options for events collection

^mongodb(/.+)?$.description: The purpose of this receiver is to allow users to monitor metrics from standalone MongoDB clusters. This includes non-Atlas managed MongoDB Servers.

hosts.description: Deprecated - Transport option will be removed in v0.102.0

^mysql(/.+)?$.description: This receiver queries MySQL's global status and InnoDB tables.

^named_pipe(/.+)?$.description: NamedPipeConfig defines configuration for the named_pipe receiver

^namedpipe(/.+)?$.description: NamedPipeConfig defines configuration for the named_pipe receiver

^netflow(/.+)?$.description: Config represents the receiver config settings within the collector's config.yaml

hostname.description: The hostname or IP address that the listener will bind to

port.description: The port that the listener will bind to

queue_size.description: The size of the queue that the listener will use This is a buffer that will hold flow messages before they are processed by a worker

scheme.description: The scheme defines the type of flow data that the listener will receive The scheme must be one of sflow, netflow, or flow

send_raw.description: SendRaw determines whether to send raw flow messages instead of parsing them

sockets.description: The number of sockets that the listener will use

workers.description: The number of workers that the listener will use to decode incoming flow messages By default it will be two times the number of sockets Ideally set this to the number of CPU cores

^nginx(/.+)?$.description: You must configure NGINX to expose status information by editing the NGINX configuration. Please see [ngx_http_stub_status_module](http://nginx.org/en/docs/http/ngx_http_stub_status_module.html) for a guide to configuring the NGINX stats module `ngx_http_stub_status_module`.

^nsxt(/.+)?$.description: Config is the configuration for the NSX receiver

^ntp(/.+)?$.description: Config is the configuration for the NSX receiver

^oracledb(/.+)?$.description: To use the Oracle DB receiver you must define how to connect to your DB. This can be done in two ways, defined in the [Primary](#primary-configuration-option) and [Secondary](#secondary-configuration-option) configuration option sections. Defining one of the two configurations is required. If both are defined, the primary option will be used.

^otlpjsonfile(/.+)?$.description: The receiver will watch the directory and read files. If a file is updated or added, the receiver will read it in its entirety again.

^otelarrow(/.+)?$.description: Config defines configuration for OTel Arrow receiver.

request_limit_mib.description: RequestLimitMiB limits the number of requests that are received by the stream based on uncompressed request size. Request size is used to control how much traffic we admit for processing.  When this field is zero, admission control is disabled.

waiting_limit_mib.description: WaitingLimitMiB is the limit on the amount of data waiting to be consumed. This is a dimension of memory limiting to ensure waiters are not consuming an unexpectedly large amount of memory in the arrow receiver.

arrow_config.description: ArrowConfig support configuring the Arrow receiver.

admission_limit_mib.description: Deprecated: This field is no longer supported, use cfg.Admission.RequestLimitMiB instead.

memory_limit_mib.description: MemoryLimitMiB is the size of a shared memory region used by all Arrow streams, in MiB.  When too much load is passing through, they will see ResourceExhausted errors.

waiter_limit.description: Deprecated: This field is no longer supported, use cfg.Admission.WaiterLimit instead.

zstd.description: Zstd settings apply to OTel-Arrow use of gRPC specifically.

protocols.description: Protocols is the configuration for the supported protocols.

admission.description: Admission is the configuration for controlling amount of request memory entering the receiver.

endpoint.description: The URL of the podman server.  Default is "unix:///run/podman/podman.sock"

^pprof(/.+)?$.description: Config defines the configuration for the pprof receiver.

block_profile_fraction.description: Self-scraper only: Fraction of blocking events that are profiled. A value <= 0 disables profiling. See https://golang.org/pkg/runtime/#SetBlockProfileRate for details.

include.description: Include is the glob pattern for pprof files to scrape.

mutex_profile_fraction.description: Self-scraper only: Fraction of mutex contention events that are profiled. A value <= 0 disables profiling. See https://golang.org/pkg/runtime/#SetMutexProfileFraction for details.

^prometheus(/.+)?$.description: Config defines configuration for Prometheus receiver.

prom_config.description: PromConfig is a redeclaration of promconfig.Config because we need custom unmarshaling as prometheus "config" uses `yaml` tags.

api_server.description: APIServer has the settings to enable the receiver to host the Prometheus API server in agent mode. This allows the user to call the endpoint to get the config, service discovery, and targets for debugging purposes.

^prometheus_remote_write(/.+)?$.description: Config holds common fields and embedded protocol-specific configurations

^prometheusremotewrite(/.+)?$.description: Config holds common fields and embedded protocol-specific configurations

^purefa(/.+)?$.description: Config relating to Array Metric Scraper.

array.description: Array represents the list of arrays to query

directories.description: Directories represents the list of directories to query

env.description: Env represents the respective environment value valid to scrape

fa_array_name.description: ArrayName represents the display name that is appended to the received metrics, as the `host` label if not provided by OpenMetrics output, and to the `fa_array_name` label always

hosts.description: Hosts represents the list of hosts to query

namespace.description: Namespace selects the OpenMetrics namespace requested

pods.description: Pods represents the list of pods to query

settings.description: Settings contains settings for the individual scrapers

volumes.description: Volumes represents the list of volumes to query

^purefb(/.+)?$.description: Config relating to Array Metric Scraper.

arrays.description: Arrays represents the list of arrays to query

clients.description: Clients represents the list of clients metrics

env.description: Env represents the respective environment value valid to scrape

settings.description: Settings contains settings for the individual scrapers

usage.description: Usage represents the list of usage to query

^pulsar(/.+)?$.description: The following settings can be optionally configured: - `endpoint` (default = pulsar://localhost:6650): The url of pulsar cluster. - `topic` (default = otlp_spans for traces, otlp_metrics for metrics, otlp_logs for logs): The name of the pulsar topic to consume from. - `encoding` (default = otlp_proto): The encoding of the payload sent to pulsar. Available encodings: - `otlp_proto`: the payload is deserialized to `ExportTraceServiceRequest`. - `jaeger_proto`: the payload is deserialized to a single Jaeger proto `Span`. - `jaeger_json`: the payload is deserialized to a single Jaeger JSON Span using `jsonpb`.

endpoint.description: Configure the service URL for the Pulsar service.

subscription.description: The Subscription that receiver will be consuming messages from (default "otlp_subscription")

tls_allow_insecure_connection.description: Configure whether the Pulsar client accept untrusted TLS certificate from broker (default: false)

tls_trust_certs_file_path.description: Set the path to the trusted TLS certificate file

topic.description: The topic of pulsar to consume logs,metrics,traces. (default = "otlp_traces" for traces, "otlp_metrics" for metrics, "otlp_logs" for logs)

^rabbitmq(/.+)?$.description: Config defines the configuration for the various elements of the receiver agent.

resource_attributes.description: ResourceAttributes is a map of default resource attributes to add to each resource object received by this receiver from dynamically created receivers.

watch_observers.description: WatchObservers are the extensions to listen to endpoints from.

^redis(/.+)?$.description: The Redis INFO command returns information and statistics about a Redis server (see [https://redis.io/commands/info](https://redis.io/commands/info) for details). The Redis receiver extracts values from the result and converts them to open telemetry metrics. Details about the metrics produced by the Redis receiver can be found by browsing [metric_functions.go](metric_functions.go).

password.description: Optional password. Must match the password specified in the requirepass server configuration option, or the user's password when connecting to a Redis 6.0 instance, or greater, that is using the Redis ACL system.

username.description: Optional username. Use the specified Username to authenticate the current connection with one of the connections defined in the ACL list when connecting to a Redis 6.0 instance, or greater, that is using the Redis ACL system.

^riak(/.+)?$.description: Config defines the configuration for the various elements of the receiver agent.

^saphana(/.+)?$.description: A monitoring user requires `SELECT` access to the relevant monitoring views. The following sql script should create a monitoring role and apply it to a monitoring user if executed by a user with sufficient permissions connected to the SAP HANA instance.

^signalfx(/.+)?$.description: Config defines configuration for the SignalFx receiver.

^prometheus_simple(/.+)?$.description: Config defines configuration for simple prometheus receiver.

collection_interval.description: CollectionInterval is the interval at which metrics should be collected

job_name.description: JobName allows users to customize the job name optionally.

metrics_path.description: MetricsPath the path to the metrics endpoint.

params.description: Params the parameters to the metrics endpoint.

ca_file.description: Path to the CA cert that has signed the TLS cert.

cert_file.description: Path to the client TLS cert to use for TLS required connections.

insecure_skip_verify.description: Whether or not to verify the exporter's TLS cert.

key_file.description: Path to the client TLS key to use for TLS required connections.

use_service_account.description: Whether or not to use pod service account to authenticate.

^skywalking(/.+)?$.description: Config defines configuration for skywalking receiver.

protocols.description: Protocols is the configuration for the supported protocols.

^solace(/.+)?$.description: Config defines configuration for Solace receiver.

flow_control.description: FlowControl defines the configuration for what to do in backpressure scenarios, e.g. memorylimiter errors

flow_control_delayed_retry.description: FlowControlDelayedRetry represents the strategy of waiting for a defined amount of time (in time.Duration) and attempt redelivery

sasl_x_auth_2_config.description: SaslXAuth2Config defines the configuration for the SASL XAUTH2 authentication.

broker.description: The list of solace brokers (default localhost:5671)

max_unacknowledged.description: The maximum number of unacknowledged messages the Solace broker can transmit, to configure AMQP Link

queue.description: The name of the solace queue to consume from, it is required parameter

^splunkenterprise(/.+)?$.description: **By default the Splunk Enterprise receiver is not configured to gather any metrics other than `splunk.health`**

^splunk_hec(/.+)?$.description: Config defines configuration for the Splunk HEC receiver.

ack.description: Ack defines configuration for the ACK functionality of the HEC receiver

extension.description: Extension defines the extension to use for acking of events. Without specifying an extension, the ACK endpoint won't be exposed

path.description: Path for Ack API, default is '/services/collector/ack'. Ignored if Extension is not provided.

health_path.description: HealthPath for health API, default is '/services/collector/health'

hec_metadata_to_otel_attrs.description: HecToOtelAttrs creates a mapping from HEC metadata to attributes.

raw_path.description: RawPath for raw data collection, default is '/services/collector/raw'

splitting.description: Splitting defines the splitting strategy used by the receiver when ingesting raw events. Can be set to "line" or "none". Default is "line".

^stef(/.+)?$.description: Config defines configuration for STEF receiver.

max_open_conn.description: The maximumn number of open connections to the sql server. <= 0 means unlimited

^sqlserver(/.+)?$.description: Config defines configuration for a sqlserver receiver.

lookback_time.description: Enabled enables the collection of the top queries by the execution time. It will collect the top N queries based on totalElapsedTimeDiffs during the last collection interval. The query statement will also be reported, hence, it is not ideal to send it as a metric. Hence we are reporting them as logs. The `N` is configured via `TopQueryCount`

^sshcheck(/.+)?$.description: If `ignore_host_key` is not set then host key validation requires the agent either have a known_hosts file at a path specified by setting `known_hosts` or at default paths indicated by ssh man pages: $HOME/.ssh/known_hosts or /etc/ssh/known_hosts.

^statsd(/.+)?$.description: Config defines configuration for StatsD receiver.

counter_type.description: CounterType specifies how counter values are represented in exported metrics. Valid values: "int" (default), "float", "stochastic_int".

socket_permissions.description: Will only be used when transport set to 'unixgram'.

^syslog(/.+)?$.description: SysLogConfig defines configuration for the syslog receiver

^systemd(/.+)?$.description: This scraper generates a metric with a label for service state with a value of `1` if the unit is in that state. For example, the following metrics will be generated if the `nginx` service is currently active:

^tcpcheck(/.+)?$.description: Config defines the configuration for the various elements of the receiver agent.

^tcp_log(/.+)?$.description: TCPLogConfig defines configuration for the tcp_log receiver

^tcplog(/.+)?$.description: TCPLogConfig defines configuration for the tcp_log receiver

^tls_check(/.+)?$.description: Config defines the configuration for the various elements of the receiver agent.

certificate_target.description: CertificateTarget represents a target for certificate checking, which can be either a network endpoint or a local file

file_format.description: FileFormat represents the format of a local certificate file.

^tlscheck(/.+)?$.description: Config defines the configuration for the various elements of the receiver agent.

certificate_target.description: CertificateTarget represents a target for certificate checking, which can be either a network endpoint or a local file

file_format.description: FileFormat represents the format of a local certificate file.

^udp_log(/.+)?$.description: UDPLogConfig defines configuration for the udp_log receiver

^udplog(/.+)?$.description: UDPLogConfig defines configuration for the udp_log receiver

^vcenter(/.+)?$.description: Config is the configuration of the receiver

^wavefront(/.+)?$.description: Config defines configuration for the Wavefront receiver.

extract_collectd_tags.description: ExtractCollectdTags instructs the Wavefront receiver to attempt to extract tags in the CollectD format from the metric name. The default is false.

tcp_idle_timeout.description: TCPIdleTimeout is the timeout for idle TCP connections.

^snmp(/.+)?$.description: Config defines the configuration for the various elements of the receiver.

attribute.description: Attribute is a connection between a metric configuration and an AttributeConfig

name.description: Name is required and should match the key for an AttributeConfig

value.description: Value is optional and is only needed for a matched AttributeConfig's with enum value. Value should match one of the AttributeConfig's enum values in this case

attribute_config.description: AttributeConfig contains config info about all of the metric attributes that will be used by this receiver.

description.description: Description is optional and describes what the attribute represents

enum.description: Enum is required only if OID and IndexedValuePrefix are not defined. This contains a list of possible values that can be associated with this attribute

indexed_value_prefix.description: IndexedValuePrefix is required only if Enum and OID are not defined. This is used alongside metrics with ColumnOIDs to assign attribute values using this prefix + the OID index of the metric value

oid.description: OID is required only if Enum and IndexedValuePrefix are not defined. This is the column OID which will provide indexed values to be used for this attribute (alongside a metric with ColumnOIDs)

value.description: Value is optional, and will allow for a different attribute key other than the attribute name

column_oid.description: ColumnOID holds OID info for an indexed metric as well as any attributes or resource attributes that are attached to it

attributes.description: Attributes is required only if there are no ResourceAttributes associated defined here. Valid values are non enum AttributeConfig names that will be used to differentiate the indexed values for the column OID

oid.description: OID is required and is the column OID that is associated with a metric

resource_attributes.description: ResourceAttributes is required only if there are no Attributes associated with non enum AttributeConfigs defined here. Valid values are ResourceAttributeConfig names that will be used to differentiate the indexed values for the column OID

gauge_metric.description: GaugeMetric contains info about the value of the gauge metric

value_type.description: ValueType is required and can be either int or double

metric_config.description: MetricConfig contains config info about a given metric

description.description: Description is optional and describes what this metric represents

gauge.description: Either Gauge or Sum config is required

scalar_oids.description: Either ScalarOIDs or ColumnOIDs is required. ScalarOIDs is used if one or more scalar OID values is used for this metric. ColumnOIDs is used if one or more column OID indexed set of values is used for this metric.

resource_attribute_config.description: ResourceAttributeConfig contains config info about all of the resource attributes that will be used by this receiver.

description.description: Description is optional and describes what the resource attribute represents

indexed_value_prefix.description: IndexedValuePrefix is required only if OID or ScalarOID is not set. This will be used alongside indexed metric values for this resource attribute. The prefix value concatenated with specific indexes of metric indexed values (Ex: prefix.1.2) will ultimately each be associated with a different "resource" as an attribute on that resource. The related indexed metric values will then be used to associate metric datapoints to those resources.

oid.description: OID is required only if ScalarOID or IndexedValuePrefix is not set. This is the column OID which will provide indexed values to be used for this resource attribute. These indexed values will ultimately each be associated with a different "resource" as an attribute on that resource. Indexed metric values will then be used to associate metric datapoints to the matching "resource" (based on matching indexes).

scalar_oid.description: ScalarOID is required only if OID or IndexedValuePrefix is not set. This is the scalar OID which will provide a value to be used for this resource attribute. Single or indexed metrics can then be associated with the resource. (Indexed metrics also need an indexed attribute or resource attribute to associate with a scalar metric resource attribute)
ScalarOID holds OID info for a scalar metric as well as any {resource} attributes that are attached to it

attributes.description: Attributes is optional and may contain names and values associated with enum AttributeConfigs to associate with the value of the scalar OID

oid.description: OID is required and is the scalar OID that is associated with a metric

resource_attributes.description: ResourceAttributes is optional and may contain only scalar OID values to associate this metric with

sum_metric.description: SumMetric contains info about the value of the sum metric

aggregation.description: Aggregation is required and can be cumulative or delta

monotonic.description: Monotonic is required and can be true or false

value_type.description: ValueType is required and can be either int or double

attributes.description: Attributes defines what attributes will be used on metrics for this receiver and is composed of attribute names along with their attribute configurations

auth_password.description: AuthPassword is the authentication password used for this SNMP connection. Only valid for version "v3" and if "no_auth_no_priv" is not selected for SecurityLevel

auth_type.description: AuthType is the type of authentication protocol to use for this SNMP connection. Only valid for version “v3” and if “no_auth_no_priv” is not selected for SecurityLevel Valid options: “md5”, “sha”, “sha224”, “sha256”, “sha384”, “sha512” Default: "md5"

community.description: Community is the SNMP community string to use. Only valid for versions "v1" and "v2c" Default: public

endpoint.description: Endpoint is the SNMP target to request data from. Must be formatted as [udp|tcp|][4|6|]://{host}:{port}. Default: udp://localhost:161 If no scheme is given, udp4 is assumed. If no port is given, 161 is assumed.

metrics.description: Metrics defines what SNMP metrics will be collected for this receiver and is composed of metric names along with their metric configurations

privacy_password.description: PrivacyPassword is the authentication password used for this SNMP connection. Only valid for version “v3” and if "auth_priv" is selected for SecurityLevel

privacy_type.description: PrivacyType is the type of privacy protocol to use for this SNMP connection. Only valid for version “v3” and if "auth_priv" is selected for SecurityLevel Valid options: “des”, “aes”, “aes192”, “aes256”, “aes192c”, “aes256c” Default: "des"

resource_attributes.description: ResourceAttributes defines what resource attributes will be used for this receiver and is composed of resource attribute names along with their resource attribute configurations

security_level.description: SecurityLevel is the security level to use for this SNMP connection. Only valid for version “v3” Valid options: “no_auth_no_priv”, “auth_no_priv”, “auth_priv” Default: "no_auth_no_priv"

user.description: User is the SNMP User for this connection. Only valid for version “v3”

version.description: Version is the version of SNMP to use for this connection. Valid options: v1, v2c, v3. Default: v2c

^webhookevent(/.+)?$.description: Config defines configuration for the Generic Webhook receiver.

^windows_event_log(/.+)?$.description: WindowsLogConfig defines configuration for the Windows Event Log receiver.

resolve_si_ds_config.description: ResolveSIDsConfig contains configuration for SID resolution

cache_size.description: CacheSize is the maximum number of SIDs to cache (LRU eviction) Default: 10000

cache_ttl.description: CacheTTL is how long cache entries remain valid Default: 15m

enabled.description: Enabled controls whether SID resolution is active

discover_domain_controllers.description: DiscoverDomainControllers controls whether to attempt auto-discovery of domain controllers for joined machines with remote credentials

resolve_sids.description: ResolveSIDs contains configuration for SID-to-username resolution

^windowseventlog(/.+)?$.description: WindowsLogConfig defines configuration for the Windows Event Log receiver.

resolve_si_ds_config.description: ResolveSIDsConfig contains configuration for SID resolution

cache_size.description: CacheSize is the maximum number of SIDs to cache (LRU eviction) Default: 10000

cache_ttl.description: CacheTTL is how long cache entries remain valid Default: 15m

enabled.description: Enabled controls whether SID resolution is active

discover_domain_controllers.description: DiscoverDomainControllers controls whether to attempt auto-discovery of domain controllers for joined machines with remote credentials

resolve_sids.description: ResolveSIDs contains configuration for SID-to-username resolution

^windowsperfcounters(/.+)?$.description: Config defines configuration for WindowsPerfCounters receiver.

counter_config.description: CounterConfig defines the individual counter in an object.

metric_config.description: MetricsConfig defines the configuration for a metric to be created.

object_config.description: ObjectConfig defines configuration for a perf counter object.

^yang_grpc(/.+)?$.description: Config defines configuration for yanggrpc receiver.

burst_size.description: BurstSize is the maximum burst size for rate limiting

cleanup_interval.description: CleanupInterval is how often to clean up rate limiter entries

enabled.description: Enabled indicates whether rate limiting should be enabled

requests_per_second.description: RequestsPerSecond is the maximum number of requests per second per client

allowed_clients.description: AllowedClients contains client IP allowlist configuration

connection_timeout.description: ConnectionTimeout is the maximum time to wait for new connections

cache_modules.description: CacheModules enables caching of discovered YANG modules

enable_rfc_parser.description: EnableRFCParser enables the RFC 6020/7950 compliant YANG parser

max_modules.description: MaxModules is the maximum number of YANG modules to cache

module_paths.description: ModulePaths defines the directories where .yang files are stored. This is used by the internal parser to resolve Cisco-specific schemas.

^yanggrpc(/.+)?$.description: Config defines configuration for yanggrpc receiver.

burst_size.description: BurstSize is the maximum burst size for rate limiting

cleanup_interval.description: CleanupInterval is how often to clean up rate limiter entries

enabled.description: Enabled indicates whether rate limiting should be enabled

requests_per_second.description: RequestsPerSecond is the maximum number of requests per second per client

allowed_clients.description: AllowedClients contains client IP allowlist configuration

connection_timeout.description: ConnectionTimeout is the maximum time to wait for new connections

cache_modules.description: CacheModules enables caching of discovered YANG modules

enable_rfc_parser.description: EnableRFCParser enables the RFC 6020/7950 compliant YANG parser

max_modules.description: MaxModules is the maximum number of YANG modules to cache

module_paths.description: ModulePaths defines the directories where .yang files are stored. This is used by the internal parser to resolve Cisco-specific schemas.

^zipkin(/.+)?$.description: Config defines configuration for Zipkin receiver.

parse_string_tags.description: If enabled the zipkin receiver will attempt to parse string tags/binary annotations into int/bool/float. Disabled by default

^zookeeper(/.+)?$.description: - `endpoint`: (default = `localhost:2181`) Endpoint to connect to collect metrics. Takes the form `host:port`. See our [security best practices doc](https://opentelemetry.io/docs/security/config-best-practices/#protect-against-denial-of-service-attacks) to understand how to set the endpoint in different environments. - `timeout`: (default = `10s`) Timeout within which requests should be completed. - `initial_delay` (default = `1s`): defines how long this receiver waits before starting.

^batch(/.+)?$.description: Config defines configuration for the batch processor — groups items together before forwarding to the next consumer.

send_batch_size.description: Number of spans, metric data points, or log records after which a batch is sent regardless of timeout. Default 8192. Should be lower than `send_batch_max_size`.

send_batch_max_size.description: Maximum allowed batch size. Items beyond this split into multiple batches. 0 = no upper bound. Must be greater than or equal to `send_batch_size`.

timeout.description: Time after which the batch is sent regardless of size. 0 disables time-based batching. Default 200ms.

metadata_keys.description: Client metadata keys whose values, when set, isolate batches. Different values create different batches. Empty (default) means client metadata is ignored.

metadata_cardinality_limit.description: Hard limit on the number of distinct combinations of `metadata_keys` values. Default 1000.

^memory_limiter(/.+)?$.description: Config defines configuration for the memory_limiter processor — refuses data and forces GC when memory limits are exceeded, protecting the collector from OOM.

check_interval.description: Frequency of memory checks. Default 0 (disabled). Common values 1s–5s. Lower values increase responsiveness at the cost of CPU.

limit_mib.description: Hard memory limit in MiB. When exceeded, the processor refuses data and force-runs GC. Mutually exclusive with `limit_percentage`. 0 = disabled.

spike_limit_mib.description: Spike allowance subtracted from `limit_mib` to derive the soft limit. Default 20% of `limit_mib`.

limit_percentage.description: Hard memory limit as a percentage of total system memory (0–100). Used in containerized environments. Mutually exclusive with `limit_mib`.

spike_limit_percentage.description: Spike allowance as a percentage of total memory. Used with `limit_percentage`.

^attributes(/.+)?$.description: Config specifies the set of attributes to be inserted, updated, upserted and deleted and the properties to include/exclude a span from being processed. This processor handles all forms of modifications to attributes within a span, log, or metric. Prior to any actions being applied, each span is compared against the include properties and then the exclude properties if they are specified. This determines if a span is to be processed or not. The list of actions is applied in order specified in the configuration.

^cumulativetodelta(/.+)?$.description: Config defines the configuration for the processor.

include.description: Include specifies a filter on the metrics that should be converted. Exclude specifies a filter on the metrics that should not be converted. If neither `include` nor `exclude` are set, all metrics will be converted. Cannot be used with deprecated Metrics config option.

initial_value.description: InitialValue determines how to handle the first datapoint for a given metric. Valid values: - auto: (default) send the first point iff the startime is set AND the starttime happens after the component started AND the starttime is different from the timestamp - keep: always send the first point - drop: don't send the first point, but store it for subsequent delta calculations

max_staleness.description: MaxStaleness is the total time a state entry will live past the time it was last seen. Set to 0 to retain state indefinitely.

^coralogix(/.+)?$.description: - `transactions`: - `enabled` (`false` by default): enables the transactions feature from the Coralogix processor (more information below).

^deltatorate(/.+)?$.description: Config defines the configuration for the processor.

metrics.description: List of delta sum metrics to convert to rates

^drain(/.+)?$.description: This processor **annotates**; it does not filter. Use the [filter processor](../filterprocessor/README.md) downstream to act on the `log.record.template` attribute — for example, to drop entire classes of noisy logs by pattern.

^filter(/.+)?$.description: Config defines configuration for Resource processor.

exclude.description: Exclude match properties describe logs that should be excluded from the Collector Service pipeline, all other logs should be included. If both Include and Exclude are specified, Include filtering occurs first.

include.description: Include match properties describe logs that should be included in the Collector Service pipeline, all other logs should be dropped from further processing. If both Include and Exclude are specified, Include filtering occurs first.

log_record.description: LogConditions is a list of OTTL conditions for an ottllog context. If any condition resolves to true, the log event will be dropped. Supports `and`, `or`, and `()`

resource.description: ResourceConditions is a list of OTTL conditions for an ottlresource context. If any condition resolves to true, the whole resource will be dropped. Supports `and`, `or`, and `()`

log_match_properties.description: LogMatchProperties specifies the set of properties in a log to match against and the type of string pattern matching to use.

bodies.description: LogBodies is a list of strings that the LogRecord's body field must match against.

match_type.description: LogMatchType specifies the type of matching desired

record_attributes.description: RecordAttributes defines a list of possible record attributes to match logs against. A match occurs if any record attribute matches at least one expression in this given list.

resource_attributes.description: ResourceAttributes defines a list of possible resource attributes to match logs against. A match occurs if any resource attribute matches all expressions in this given list.

severity_number.description: SeverityNumberProperties defines how to match against a log record's SeverityNumber, if defined.

severity_texts.description: SeverityTexts is a list of strings that the LogRecord's severity text field must match against.

log_match_type.description: LogMatchType specifies the strategy for matching against `plog.Log`s.

match_undefined.description: MatchUndefined lets logs records with "unknown" severity match. If MinSeverity is not set, this field is ignored, as fields are not matched based on severity.

min.description: Min is the minimum severity needed for the log record to match. This corresponds to the short names specified here: https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-specification/blob/main/specification/logs/data-model.md#displaying-severity this field is case-insensitive ("INFO" == "info")

datapoint.description: DataPointConditions is a list of OTTL conditions for an ottldatapoint context. If any condition resolves to true, the datapoint will be dropped. Supports `and`, `or`, and `()`

exclude.description: Exclude match properties describe metrics that should be excluded from the Collector Service pipeline, all other metrics should be included. If both Include and Exclude are specified, Include filtering occurs first.

include.description: Include match properties describe metrics that should be included in the Collector Service pipeline, all other metrics should be dropped from further processing. If both Include and Exclude are specified, Include filtering occurs first.

metric.description: MetricConditions is a list of OTTL conditions for an ottlmetric context. If any condition resolves to true, the metric will be dropped. Supports `and`, `or`, and `()`

regexp.description: RegexpConfig specifies options for the regexp match type

resource.description: ResourceConditions is a list of OTTL conditions for an ottlresource context. If any condition resolves to true, the whole resource will be dropped. Supports `and`, `or`, and `()`

profile.description: ProfileConditions is a list of OTTL conditions for an ottlprofile context. If any condition resolves to true, the profile will be dropped. Supports `and`, `or`, and `()`

resource.description: ResourceConditions is a list of OTTL conditions for an ottlresource context. If any condition resolves to true, the whole resource will be dropped. Supports `and`, `or`, and `()`
ResourceConditions is a list of OTTL conditions for an ottlresource context. If any condition resolves to true, the whole resource will be dropped. Supports `and`, `or`, and `()`

span.description: SpanConditions is a list of OTTL conditions for an ottlspan context. If any condition resolves to true, the span will be dropped. Supports `and`, `or`, and `()`

spanevent.description: SpanEventConditions is a list of OTTL conditions for an ottlspanevent context. If any condition resolves to true, the span event will be dropped. Supports `and`, `or`, and `()`

error_mode.description: ErrorMode determines how the processor reacts to errors that occur while processing an OTTL condition. Valid values are `ignore` and `propagate`. `ignore` means the processor ignores errors returned by conditions and continues on to the next condition. This is the recommended mode. `propagate` means the processor returns the error up the pipeline.  This will result in the payload being dropped from the collector. The default value is `propagate`. It will change to `ignore` when the `processor.filter.defaultErrorModeIgnore` feature gate is stable.

^geoip(/.+)?$.description: Config holds the configuration for the GeoIP processor.

attributes.description: An array of attribute names, which are used for the IP address lookup

context.description: Context section allows specifying the source type to look for the IP. Available options: resource or record.

^groupbyattrs(/.+)?$.description: Config is the configuration for the processor.

keys.description: GroupByKeys describes the attribute names that are going to be used for grouping. Empty value is allowed, since processor in such case can compact data

^groupbytrace(/.+)?$.description: Config is the configuration for the processor.

discard_orphans.description: DiscardOrphans instructs the processor to discard traces without the root span. This typically indicates that the trace is incomplete. Default: false. Not yet implemented, and an error will be returned when this option is used.

num_traces.description: NumTraces is the max number of traces to keep in memory waiting for the duration. Default: 1_000_000.

num_workers.description: NumWorkers is a number of workers processing event queue. Default: 1.

store_on_disk.description: StoreOnDisk tells the processor to keep only the trace ID in memory, serializing the trace spans to disk. Useful when the duration to wait for traces to complete is high. Default: false. Not yet implemented, and an error will be returned when this option is used.

wait_duration.description: WaitDuration tells the processor to wait for the specified duration for the trace to be complete. Default: 1s.

^interval(/.+)?$.description: Config defines the configuration for the processor.

gauge.description: Gauge is a flag that determines whether gauge metrics should be passed through as they are or aggregated.

summary.description: Summary is a flag that determines whether summary metrics should be passed through as they are or aggregated.

interval.description: Interval is the time interval at which the processor will aggregate metrics.

pass_through.description: PassThrough is a configuration that determines whether gauge and summary metrics should be passed through as they are or aggregated.

^isolationforest(/.+)?$.description: Config represents the configuration for the isolation forest processor.

adaptive_window_config.description: AdaptiveWindowConfig configures automatic window size adjustment based on traffic patterns

enabled.description: Core configuration

^k8s_attributes(/.+)?$.description: Config defines configuration for k8s attributes processor.

exclude_config.description: ExcludeConfig represent a list of Pods to exclude

exclude_pod_config.description: ExcludePodConfig represent a Pod name to ignore

extract_config.description: ExtractConfig section allows specifying extraction rules to extract data from k8s pod specs.

annotations.description: Annotations allows extracting data from pod annotations and record it as resource attributes. It is a list of FieldExtractConfig type. See FieldExtractConfig documentation for more details.

deployment_name_from_replicaset.description: DeploymentNameFromReplicaSet allows extracting deployment name from replicaset name by trimming pod template hash. This will disable watching for replicaset resources.

labels.description: Labels allows extracting data from pod labels and record it as resource attributes. It is a list of FieldExtractConfig type. See FieldExtractConfig documentation for more details.

metadata.description: Metadata allows to extract pod/namespace/node metadata from a list of metadata fields. The field accepts a list of strings. Metadata fields supported right now are, k8s.pod.name, k8s.pod.uid, k8s.deployment.name, k8s.node.name, k8s.namespace.name, k8s.pod.start_time, k8s.replicaset.name, k8s.replicaset.uid, k8s.daemonset.name, k8s.daemonset.uid, k8s.job.name, k8s.job.uid, k8s.cronjob.name, k8s.cronjob.uid, k8s.statefulset.name, k8s.statefulset.uid, k8s.container.name, container.id, container.image.name, container.image.tag, container.image.repo_digests k8s.cluster.uid Specifying anything other than these values will result in an error. By default, the following fields are extracted and added to spans, metrics and logs as resource attributes: - k8s.pod.name - k8s.pod.uid - k8s.pod.start_time - k8s.namespace.name - k8s.node.name - k8s.deployment.name (if the pod is controlled by a deployment) - k8s.container.name (requires an additional attribute to be set: container.id) - container.image.name (requires one of the following additional attributes to be set: container.id or k8s.container.name) - container.image.tag (requires one of the following additional attributes to be set: container.id or k8s.container.name)

otel_annotations.description: OtelAnnotations extracts all pod annotations with the prefix "resource.opentelemetry.io" as resource attributes E.g. "resource.opentelemetry.io/foo" becomes "foo"

field_extract_config.description: FieldExtractConfig allows specifying an extraction rule to extract a resource attribute from pod (or namespace) annotations (or labels).

from.description: From represents the source of the labels/annotations. Allowed values are "pod", "namespace", and "node". The default is pod.

key.description: Key represents the annotation (or label) name. This must exactly match an annotation (or label) name.

key_regex.description: KeyRegex is a regular expression used to extract a Key that matches the regex. Out of Key or KeyRegex, only one option is expected to be configured at a time.

tag_name.description: TagName represents the name of the resource attribute that will be added to logs, metrics or spans. When not specified, a default tag name will be used of the format: - k8s.pod.annotations.<annotation key>  (or k8s.pod.annotation.<annotation key> when processor.k8sattributes.EmitV1K8sConventions is enabled) - k8s.pod.labels.<label key>  (or k8s.pod.label.<label key> when processor.k8sattributes.EmitV1K8sConventions is enabled) For example, if tag_name is not specified and the key is git_sha, then the attribute name will be `k8s.pod.annotations.git_sha` (or `k8s.pod.annotation.git_sha` with the feature gate). When key_regex is present, tag_name supports back reference to both named capturing and positioned capturing. For example, if your pod spec contains the following labels, app.kubernetes.io/component: mysql app.kubernetes.io/version: 5.7.21 and you'd like to add tags for all labels with prefix app.kubernetes.io/ and also trim the prefix, then you can specify the following extraction rules: extract: labels: - tag_name: $$1 key_regex: kubernetes.io/(.*) this will add the `component` and `version` tags to the spans or metrics. When key_regex is present without tag_name, the default tag name format will be used for each matched key. For example: extract: labels: - key_regex: environment\.(.*) from: pod If labels like "environment.prod" and "environment.dev" exist, they will be extracted as k8s.pod.labels.environment.prod and k8s.pod.labels.environment.dev respectively.

field_filter_config.description: FieldFilterConfig allows specifying exactly one filter by a field. It can be used to represent a label or generic field filter.

key.description: Key represents the key or name of the field or labels that a filter can apply on.

op.description: Op represents the filter operation to apply on the given Key: Value pair. The following operations are supported equals, not-equals, exists, does-not-exist.

value.description: Value represents the value associated with the key that a filter operation specified by the `Op` field applies on.

filter_config.description: FilterConfig section allows specifying filters to filter pods by labels, fields, namespaces, nodes, etc.

fields.description: Fields allows to filter pods by generic k8s fields. Only the following operations are supported: - equals - not-equals Check FieldFilterConfig for more details.

labels.description: Labels allows to filter pods by generic k8s pod labels. Only the following operations are supported: - equals - not-equals - exists - not-exists Check FieldFilterConfig for more details.

namespace.description: Namespace filters all pods by the provided namespace. All other pods are ignored.

node.description: Node represents a k8s node or host. If specified, any pods not running on the specified node will be ignored by the tagger.

node_from_env_var.description: NodeFromEnv can be used to extract the node name from an environment variable. The value must be the name of the environment variable. This is useful when the node a Otel agent will run on cannot be predicted. In such cases, the Kubernetes downward API can be used to add the node name to each pod as an environment variable. K8s tagger can then read this value and filter pods by it. For example, node name can be passed to each agent with the downward API as follows env: - name: K8S_NODE_NAME valueFrom: fieldRef: fieldPath: spec.nodeName Then the NodeFromEnv field can be set to `K8S_NODE_NAME` to filter all pods by the node that the agent is running on. More on downward API here: https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/inject-data-application/environment-variable-expose-pod-information/

pod_association_config.description: PodAssociationConfig contain single rule how to associate Pod metadata with logs, spans and metrics

sources.description: List of pod association sources which should be taken to identify pod

from.description: From represents the source of the association. Allowed values are "connection" and "resource_attribute".

name.description: Name represents extracted key name. e.g. ip, pod_uid, k8s.pod.ip

exclude.description: Exclude section allows to define names of pod that should be ignored while tagging.

extract.description: Extract section allows specifying extraction rules to extract data from k8s pod specs

filter.description: Filter section allows specifying filters to filter pods by labels, fields, namespaces, nodes, etc.

passthrough.description: Passthrough mode only annotates resources with the pod IP and does not try to extract any other metadata. It does not need access to the K8S cluster API. Agent/Collector must receive spans directly from services to be able to correctly detect the pod IPs.

pod_association.description: Association section allows to define rules for tagging spans, metrics, and logs with Pod metadata.

wait_for_metadata.description: WaitForMetadata is a flag that determines if the processor should wait k8s metadata to be synced when starting.

wait_for_metadata_timeout.description: WaitForMetadataTimeout is the maximum time the processor will wait for the k8s metadata to be synced.

^k8sattributes(/.+)?$.description: Config defines configuration for k8s attributes processor.

exclude_config.description: ExcludeConfig represent a list of Pods to exclude

exclude_pod_config.description: ExcludePodConfig represent a Pod name to ignore

extract_config.description: ExtractConfig section allows specifying extraction rules to extract data from k8s pod specs.

annotations.description: Annotations allows extracting data from pod annotations and record it as resource attributes. It is a list of FieldExtractConfig type. See FieldExtractConfig documentation for more details.

deployment_name_from_replicaset.description: DeploymentNameFromReplicaSet allows extracting deployment name from replicaset name by trimming pod template hash. This will disable watching for replicaset resources.

labels.description: Labels allows extracting data from pod labels and record it as resource attributes. It is a list of FieldExtractConfig type. See FieldExtractConfig documentation for more details.

Metadata fields supported right now are, k8s.pod.name, k8s.pod.uid, k8s.deployment.name, k8s.node.name, k8s.namespace.name, k8s.pod.start_time, k8s.replicaset.name, k8s.replicaset.uid, k8s.daemonset.name, k8s.daemonset.uid, k8s.job.name, k8s.job.uid, k8s.cronjob.name, k8s.cronjob.uid, k8s.statefulset.name, k8s.statefulset.uid, k8s.container.name, container.id, container.image.name, container.image.tag, container.image.repo_digests k8s.cluster.uid Specifying anything other than these values will result in an error. By default, the following fields are extracted and added to spans, metrics and logs as resource attributes: - k8s.pod.name - k8s.pod.uid - k8s.pod.start_time - k8s.namespace.name - k8s.node.name - k8s.deployment.name (if the pod is controlled by a deployment) - k8s.container.name (requires an additional attribute to be set: container.id) - container.image.name (requires one of the following additional attributes to be set: container.id or k8s.container.name) - container.image.tag (requires one of the following additional attributes to be set: container.id or k8s.container.name)

sources.description: List of pod association sources which should be taken to identify pod

from.description: From represents the source of the association. Allowed values are "connection" and "resource_attribute".

name.description: Name represents extracted key name. e.g. ip, pod_uid, k8s.pod.ip

exclude.description: Exclude section allows to define names of pod that should be ignored while tagging.

extract.description: Extract section allows specifying extraction rules to extract data from k8s pod specs

filter.description: Filter section allows specifying filters to filter pods by labels, fields, namespaces, nodes, etc.

passthrough.description: Passthrough mode only annotates resources with the pod IP and does not try to extract any other metadata. It does not need access to the K8S cluster API. Agent/Collector must receive spans directly from services to be able to correctly detect the pod IPs.

pod_association.description: Association section allows to define rules for tagging spans, metrics, and logs with Pod metadata.

wait_for_metadata.description: WaitForMetadata is a flag that determines if the processor should wait k8s metadata to be synced when starting.

wait_for_metadata_timeout.description: WaitForMetadataTimeout is the maximum time the processor will wait for the k8s metadata to be synced.

^logdedup(/.+)?$.description: Config is the config of the processor.

^metricsgeneration(/.+)?$.description: Config defines the configuration for the processor.

metric1.description: First operand metric to use in the calculation. This is a required field.

metric2.description: Second operand metric to use in the calculation. A required field if the type is calculate.

name.description: Name of the new metric being generated. This is a required field.

operation.description: The arithmetic operation to apply for the calculation. This is a required field.

scale_by.description: A constant number by which the first operand will be scaled. A required field if the type is scale.

type.description: The rule type following which the new metric will be generated. This is a required field.

unit.description: Unit for the new metric being generated.

rules.description: Set of rules for generating new metrics

^metric_start_time(/.+)?$.description: Config holds configuration of the metric start time processor.

gc_interval.description: GCInterval specifies how long to wait before removing a metric from the cache.

start_time_metric_regex.description: StartTimeMetricRegex allows specifying alternate metrics to determine start time using a regular expression. It only applies when the `start_time_metric strategy` is used.

^metricstarttime(/.+)?$.description: Config holds configuration of the metric start time processor.

gc_interval.description: GCInterval specifies how long to wait before removing a metric from the cache.

start_time_metric_regex.description: StartTimeMetricRegex allows specifying alternate metrics to determine start time using a regular expression. It only applies when the `start_time_metric strategy` is used.

^metricstransform(/.+)?$.description: Config defines configuration for Resource processor.

config_action.description: ConfigAction is the enum to capture the type of action to perform on a metric.

transforms.description: transform specifies a list of transforms on metrics with each transform focusing on one metric.

action.description: Action specifies the action performed on the matched metric. Action specifies if the operations (specified below) are performed on metrics in place (update), on an inserted clone (insert), or on a new combined metric that includes all data points from the set of matching metrics (combine). REQUIRED

aggregation_type.description: AggregationType specifies how to aggregate. REQUIRED only if Action is COMBINE.

experimental_match_labels.description: MatchLabels specifies the label set against which the metric filter will work. This field is optional.

group_resource_labels.description: GroupResourceLabels specifies resource labels that will be appended to this group's new ResourceMetrics message REQUIRED only if Action is GROUP

include.description: Include specifies the metric(s) to operate on.

match_type.description: MatchType determines how the Include string is matched: <strict|regexp>.

new_name.description: NewName specifies the name of the new metric when inserting or updating. REQUIRED only if Action is INSERT.

operations.description: Operations contains a list of operations that will be performed on the resulting metric(s).

action.description: Action specifies the action performed for this operation. REQUIRED

aggregated_values.description: AggregatedValues is a list of label values to aggregate away.

experimental_scale.description: Scale is a scalar to multiply the values with.

label.description: Label identifies the exact label to operate on.

label_set.description: LabelSet is a list of labels to keep. All other labels are aggregated based on the AggregationType.

label_value.description: LabelValue identifies the exact label value to operate on

new_label.description: NewLabel determines the name to rename the identified label to.

new_value.description: NewValue is used to set a new label value either when the operation is `AggregatedValues` or `addLabel`.

value_actions.description: ValueActions is a list of renaming actions for label values.

new_value.description: NewValue specifies the label value to rename to.

value.description: Value specifies the current label value.

submatch_case.description: SubmatchCase specifies what case to use for label values created from regexp submatches.

^probabilistic_sampler(/.+)?$.description: Config has the configuration guiding the sampler processor.

sampler_mode.description: SamplerMode determines which of several modes is used for the sampling decision.

fail_closed.description: FailClosed indicates to not sample data (the processor will fail "closed") in case of error, such as failure to parse the tracestate field or missing the randomness attribute. By default, failure cases are sampled (the processor is fails "open").  Sampling priority-based decisions are made after FailClosed is processed, making it possible to sample despite errors using priority.

from_attribute.description: FromAttribute (logs only) The optional name of a log record attribute used for sampling purposes, such as a unique log record ID. The value of the attribute is only used if the trace ID is absent or if `attribute_source` is set to `record`.

hash_seed.description: HashSeed allows one to configure the hashing seed. This is important in scenarios where multiple layers of collectors have different sampling rates: if they use the same seed all passing one layer may pass the other even if they have different sampling rates, configuring different seeds avoids that.

mode.description: Mode selects the sampling behavior. Supported values: - "hash_seed": the legacy behavior of this processor. Using an FNV hash combined with the HashSeed value, this sampler performs a non-consistent probabilistic downsampling.  The number of spans output is expected to equal SamplingPercentage (as a ratio) times the number of spans inpout, assuming good behavior from FNV and good entropy in the hashed attributes or TraceID. - "equalizing": Using an OTel-specified consistent sampling mechanism, this sampler selectively reduces the effective sampling probability of arriving spans.  This can be useful to select a small fraction of complete traces from a stream with mixed sampling rates.  The rate of spans passing through depends on how much sampling has already been applied.  If an arriving span was head sampled at the same probability it passes through.  If the span arrives with lower probability, a warning is logged because it means this sampler is configured with too large a sampling probability to ensure complete traces. - "proportional": Using an OTel-specified consistent sampling mechanism, this sampler reduces the effective sampling probability of each span by `SamplingProbability`.

sampling_percentage.description: SamplingPercentage is the percentage rate at which traces or logs are going to be sampled. Defaults to zero, i.e.: no sample. Values greater or equal 100 are treated as "sample all traces/logs".

sampling_precision.description: SamplingPrecision is how many hex digits of sampling threshold will be encoded, from 1 up to 14.  Default is 4. 0 is treated as full precision.

sampling_priority.description: SamplingPriority (logs only) enables using a log record attribute as the sampling priority of the log record.

allow_all_keys.description: AllowAllKeys is a flag to allow all span attribute keys. Setting this to true disables the AllowedKeys list. The list of BlockedValues is applied regardless. If you just want to block values, set this to true.

allowed_keys.description: AllowedKeys is a list of allowed span attribute keys. Span attributes not on the list are removed. The list fails closed if it's empty. To allow all keys, you should explicitly set AllowAllKeys

allowed_values.description: AllowedValues is a list of regular expressions for allowing values of blocked span attributes. Values that match are not masked.

blocked_key_patterns.description: BlockedKeyPatterns is a list of blocked span attribute key patterns. Span attributes matching the regexes on the list are masked.

blocked_values.description: BlockedValues is a list of regular expressions for blocking values of allowed span attributes. Values that match are masked.

db_sanitizer.description: DBSanitizer is a flag to enable database query sanitization.

hash_function.description: HashFunction defines the function for hashing the values instead of masking them with a fixed string. By default, no hash function is used and masking with a fixed string is performed.

hmac_key.description: HMACKey is the secret key used for HMAC hashing when HashFunction is set to hmac-sha256 or hmac-sha512. This should be loaded from a secure source like environment variables. Minimum length: 32 bytes for HMAC-SHA256, 64 bytes for HMAC-SHA512.

ignored_key_patterns.description: IgnoredKeyPatterns is a list of attribute key patterns (regex) that are not redacted. attributes matching any of these patterns are allowed to pass through the filter without their values being checked or modified.

ignored_keys.description: IgnoredKeys is a list of span attribute keys that are not redacted. Span attributes in this list are allowed to pass through the filter without being changed or removed.

redact_all_types.description: RedactAllTypes of attributes, including those that are not string, by converting to a string representation. By default only string values are redacted.

summary.description: Summary controls the verbosity level of the diagnostic attributes that the processor adds to the spans when it redacts or masks other attributes. In some contexts a list of redacted attributes leaks information, while it is valuable when integrating and testing a new configuration. Possible values are `debug`, `info`, and `silent`.

url_sanitizer.description: URLSanitization is a flag to sanitize URLs by removing UUIDs, timestamps, and other non-essential information

^remotetap(/.+)?$.description: The Remote Tap processor, which can be positioned anywhere in a pipeline, allows data to pass through to the next component. Simultaneously, it makes a portion of the data accessible to WebSocket clients connecting on a configurable port. This functionality resembles that of the Unix `tee` command, which enables data to flow through while duplicating and redirecting it for inspection.

limit.description: Limit is a float that indicates the maximum number of messages repeated through the websocket by this processor in messages per second. Defaults to 1.

^resourcedetection(/.+)?$.description: Config defines configuration for Resource processor.

detector_config.description: DetectorConfig contains user-specified configurations unique to all individual detectors

akamai.description: AkamaiConfig contains user-specified configurations for the akamai detector

aks.description: Aks contains user-specified configurations for the aks detector

alibaba_ecs.description: AlibabaECSConfig contains user-specified configurations for the Alibaba Cloud ECS detector

azure.description: Azure contains user-specified configurations for the azure detector

consul.description: ConsulConfig contains user-specified configurations for the Consul detector

digitalocean.description: DigitalOceanConfig contains user-specified configurations for the digitalocean detector

docker.description: DockerConfig contains user-specified configurations for the docker detector

ec2.description: EC2Config contains user-specified configurations for the EC2 detector

ecs.description: ECSConfig contains user-specified configurations for the ECS detector

eks.description: EKSConfig contains user-specified configurations for the EKS detector

elasticbeanstalk.description: Elasticbeanstalk contains user-specified configurations for the elasticbeanstalk detector

gcp.description: GcpConfig contains user-specified configurations for the gcp detector

heroku.description: HerokuConfig contains user-specified configurations for the heroku detector

hetzner.description: HetznerConfig contains user-specified configurations for the hetzner detector

ibmcloud_classic.description: IBMCloudClassicConfig contains user-specified configurations for the IBM Cloud Classic detector

ibmcloud_vpc.description: IBMCloudVPCConfig contains user-specified configurations for the IBM Cloud VPC detector

k8snode.description: K8SNode contains user-specified configurations for the K8SNode detector

kubeadm.description: Kubeadm contains user-specified configurations for the Kubeadm detector

lambda.description: Lambda contains user-specified configurations for the lambda detector

nova.description: OpenStackNovaConfig contains user-specified configurations for the OpenStackNova detector

openshift.description: OpenShift contains user-specified configurations for the OpenShift detector

oraclecloud.description: OracleCloud contains user-specified configurations for the OracleCloud detector

scaleway.description: ScalewayConfig contains user-specified configurations for the scaleway detector

system.description: SystemConfig contains user-specified configurations for the System detector

tencent_cvm.description: TencentCVMConfig contains user-specified configurations for the Tencent Cloud CVM detector

upcloud.description: UpcloudConfig contains user-specified configurations for the upcloud detector

vultr.description: VultrConfig contains user-specified configurations for the vultr detector

detectors.description: Detectors is an ordered list of named detectors that should be run to attempt to detect resource information.

override.description: Override indicates whether any existing resource attributes should be overridden or preserved. Defaults to true.

refresh_interval.description: If > 0, periodically re-run detection for all configured detectors. When 0 (default), no periodic refresh occurs.

^resource(/.+)?$.description: Config defines configuration for Resource processor.

attributes.description: AttributesActions specifies the list of actions to be applied on resource attributes. The set of actions are {INSERT, UPDATE, UPSERT, DELETE, HASH, EXTRACT}.

^schema(/.+)?$.description: Config defines the user provided values for the Schema Processor

prefetch.description: PreCache is a list of schema URLs that are downloaded and cached at the start of the collector runtime in order to avoid fetching data that later on could block processing of signals. (Optional field)

targets.description: Targets define what schema families should be translated to, allowing older and newer formats to conform to the target schema identifier.

^span(/.+)?$.description: Config is the configuration for the span processor. Prior to any actions being applied, each span is compared against the include properties and then the exclude properties if they are specified. This determines if a span is to be processed or not.

name.description: Name specifies the attributes to use to re-name a span.

from_attributes.description: FromAttributes represents the attribute keys to pull the values from to generate the new span name. All attribute keys are required in the span to re-name a span. If any attribute is missing from the span, no re-name will occur. Note: The new span name is constructed in order of the `from_attributes` specified in the configuration. This field is required and cannot be empty.

separator.description: Separator is the string used to separate attributes values in the new span name. If no value is set, no separator is used between attribute values. Used with FromAttributes only.

to_attributes.description: ToAttributes specifies a configuration to extract attributes from span name.

code.description: Code is one of three values "Ok" or "Error" or "Unset". Please check: https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-specification/blob/main/specification/trace/api.md#set-status

description.description: Description is an optional field documenting Error statuses.

to_attributes.description: ToAttributes specifies a configuration to extract attributes from span name.

break_after_match.description: BreakAfterMatch specifies if processing of rules should stop after the first match. If it is false rule processing will continue to be performed over the modified span name.

keep_original_name.description: KeepOriginalName specifies if the original span name should be kept after processing the rules. If it is true the original span name will be kept, otherwise it will be replaced with the placeholders of the captured attributes.

rules.description: Rules is a list of rules to extract attribute values from span name. The values in the span name are replaced by extracted attribute names. Each rule in the list is a regex pattern string. Span name is checked against the regex. If it matches then all named subexpressions of the regex are extracted as attributes and are added to the span. Each subexpression name becomes an attribute name and subexpression matched portion becomes the attribute value. The matched portion in the span name is replaced by extracted attribute name. If the attributes already exist in the span then they will be overwritten. The process is repeated for all rules in the order they are specified. Each subsequent rule works on the span name that is the output after processing the previous rule.

name.description: Rename specifies the components required to re-name a span. The `from_attributes` field needs to be set for this processor to be properly configured. Note: The field name is `Rename` to avoid collision with the Name() method from config.NamedEntity

status.description: SetStatus specifies status which should be set for this span.

^sumologic(/.+)?$.description: The Sumo Logic processor (config name: `sumologic`) modifies the metadata on logs, metrics and traces sent to [Sumo Logic][sumologic_webpage] so that the Sumo Logic [apps][sumologic_apps] can make full use of the ingested data.

^tail_sampling(/.+)?$.description: Config holds the configuration for tail-based sampling.

and_cfg.description: AndCfg holds the common configuration to all and policies.

and_sub_policy_cfg.description: AndSubPolicyCfg holds the common configuration to all policies under and policy.

ExtensionCfg.description: Configs for any extensions that are used.

boolean_attribute.description: Configs for boolean attribute filter sampling policy evaluator.

bytes_limiting.description: Configs for bytes limiting filter sampling policy evaluator.

latency.description: Configs for latency filter sampling policy evaluator.

name.description: Name given to the instance of the policy to make easy to identify it in metrics and logs.

numeric_attribute.description: Configs for numeric attribute filter sampling policy evaluator.

ottl_condition.description: Configs for OTTL condition filter sampling policy evaluator

probabilistic.description: Configs for probabilistic sampling policy evaluator.

rate_limiting.description: Configs for rate limiting filter sampling policy evaluator.

span_count.description: Configs for span count filter sampling policy evaluator.

status_code.description: Configs for status code filter sampling policy evaluator.

string_attribute.description: Configs for string attribute filter sampling policy evaluator.

type.description: Type of the policy this will be used to match the proper configuration of the policy.

boolean_attribute_cfg.description: BooleanAttributeCfg holds the configurable settings to create a boolean attribute filter sampling policy evaluator.

invert_match.description: InvertMatch indicates that values must not match against attribute values. If InvertMatch is true and Values is equal to 'true', all other values will be sampled except 'true'. Also, if the specified Key does not match any resource or span attributes, data will be sampled.

key.description: Tag that the filter is going to be matching against.

value.description: Value indicate the bool value, either true or false to use when matching against attribute values. BooleanAttribute Policy will apply exact value match on Value

bytes_limiting_cfg.description: BytesLimitingCfg holds the configurable settings to create a bytes limiting sampling policy evaluator using a token bucket algorithm.

burst_capacity.description: BurstCapacity sets the maximum burst capacity in bytes. If not specified, defaults to 2x BytesPerSecond. This allows for short bursts of traffic above the sustained rate. It also acts as a limit for individual trace sizes, a single trace larger than the burst size will not pass.

bytes_per_second.description: BytesPerSecond sets the limit on the maximum number of bytes that can be processed each second.

composite_cfg.description: CompositeCfg holds the configurable settings to create a composite sampling policy evaluator.

composite_sub_policy_cfg.description: CompositeSubPolicyCfg holds the common configuration to all policies under composite policy.

ExtensionCfg.description: Configs for any extensions that are used.

boolean_attribute.description: Configs for boolean attribute filter sampling policy evaluator.

bytes_limiting.description: Configs for bytes limiting filter sampling policy evaluator.

latency.description: Configs for latency filter sampling policy evaluator.

name.description: Name given to the instance of the policy to make easy to identify it in metrics and logs.

numeric_attribute.description: Configs for numeric attribute filter sampling policy evaluator.

ottl_condition.description: Configs for OTTL condition filter sampling policy evaluator

probabilistic.description: Configs for probabilistic sampling policy evaluator.

rate_limiting.description: Configs for rate limiting filter sampling policy evaluator.

span_count.description: Configs for span count filter sampling policy evaluator.

status_code.description: Configs for status code filter sampling policy evaluator.

string_attribute.description: Configs for string attribute filter sampling policy evaluator.

type.description: Type of the policy this will be used to match the proper configuration of the policy.

non_sampled_cache_size.description: NonSampledCacheSize specifies the size of the cache that holds the non-sampled trace IDs. This value will be the maximum amount of trace IDs that the cache can hold before overwriting previous IDs. For effective use, this value should be at least an order of magnitude greater than Config.NumTraces. If left as default 0, a no-op DecisionCache will be used.

sampled_cache_size.description: SampledCacheSize specifies the size of the cache that holds the sampled trace IDs. This value will be the maximum amount of trace IDs that the cache can hold before overwriting previous IDs. For effective use, this value should be at least an order of magnitude greater than Config.NumTraces. If left as default 0, a no-op DecisionCache will be used.

drop_cfg.description: DropCfg holds the common configuration to all policies under drop policy.

latency_cfg.description: LatencyCfg holds the configurable settings to create a latency filter sampling policy evaluator

threshold_ms.description: Lower bound in milliseconds. Retaining original name for compatibility

not_cfg.description: NotCfg holds the configuration for the not policy.

not_sub_policy_cfg.description: NotSubPolicyCfg holds the common configuration to the policy under the not policy.

ExtensionCfg.description: Configs for any extensions that are used.

boolean_attribute.description: Configs for boolean attribute filter sampling policy evaluator.

bytes_limiting.description: Configs for bytes limiting filter sampling policy evaluator.

latency.description: Configs for latency filter sampling policy evaluator.

name.description: Name given to the instance of the policy to make easy to identify it in metrics and logs.

numeric_attribute.description: Configs for numeric attribute filter sampling policy evaluator.

ottl_condition.description: Configs for OTTL condition filter sampling policy evaluator

probabilistic.description: Configs for probabilistic sampling policy evaluator.

rate_limiting.description: Configs for rate limiting filter sampling policy evaluator.

span_count.description: Configs for span count filter sampling policy evaluator.

status_code.description: Configs for status code filter sampling policy evaluator.

string_attribute.description: Configs for string attribute filter sampling policy evaluator.

type.description: Type of the policy this will be used to match the proper configuration of the policy.

numeric_attribute_cfg.description: NumericAttributeCfg holds the configurable settings to create a numeric attribute filter sampling policy evaluator.

invert_match.description: InvertMatch indicates that values must not match against attribute values. If InvertMatch is true and Values is equal to '123', all other values will be sampled except '123'. Also, if the specified Key does not match any resource or span attributes, data will be sampled.

key.description: Tag that the filter is going to be matching against.

max_value.description: MaxValue is the maximum value of the attribute to be considered a match.

min_value.description: MinValue is the minimum value of the attribute to be considered a match.

ottl_condition_cfg.description: OTTLConditionCfg holds the configurable setting to create a OTTL condition filter sampling policy evaluator.

policy_cfg.description: PolicyCfg holds the common configuration to all policies.

ExtensionCfg.description: Configs for any extensions that are used.

boolean_attribute.description: Configs for boolean attribute filter sampling policy evaluator.

bytes_limiting.description: Configs for bytes limiting filter sampling policy evaluator.

latency.description: Configs for latency filter sampling policy evaluator.

name.description: Name given to the instance of the policy to make easy to identify it in metrics and logs.

numeric_attribute.description: Configs for numeric attribute filter sampling policy evaluator.

ottl_condition.description: Configs for OTTL condition filter sampling policy evaluator

probabilistic.description: Configs for probabilistic sampling policy evaluator.

rate_limiting.description: Configs for rate limiting filter sampling policy evaluator.

span_count.description: Configs for span count filter sampling policy evaluator.

status_code.description: Configs for status code filter sampling policy evaluator.

string_attribute.description: Configs for string attribute filter sampling policy evaluator.

type.description: Type of the policy this will be used to match the proper configuration of the policy.

policy_type.description: PolicyType indicates the type of sampling policy.

probabilistic_cfg.description: ProbabilisticCfg holds the configurable settings to create a probabilistic sampling policy evaluator.
